title: A Dream
content:
My dead love came to me, and said,
        'God gives me one hour's rest,
    To spend with thee on earth again:
        How shall we spend it best?'

    'Why, as of old,' I said; and so
        We quarrell'd, as of old:
    But, when I turn'd to make my peace,
        That one short hour was told.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Stephen Phillips, titled "A Dream", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
